title: Inbound Gmail triage watcher: new 'email' watcher kind polling unread mail into the analyze pipeline
current_state: Gmail tools (tools/tools_gmail.py: gmail_inbox, gmail_search, gmail_read, gmail_send) are pull-only - they fire only when the user asks. There is no inbound triage: an important unread email sits unnoticed unless Ash checks manually. agent/watchers.py WatcherManager (class ~line 251) already supports file/url/schedule/keyword/price watcher kinds on a 60s poll loop with SQLite state (data/watchers.db), Telegram alert delivery, and an _analyzed_fire path (T-206) that routes events through a dedicated 'watchers' conversation with Groq analysis at a 6/hour cap.
target_state: A new watcher kind 'email': _check_email polls gmail_search('is:unread newer_than:1d'), diffs message ids against a seen-set persisted in the existing watcher state, and fires each new message through _analyzed_fire so Pi summarizes it (Groq - free tier per cost policy) and alerts via Telegram. Registered via 'watcher add email ...' like every other kind. Pure wiring: no new dependencies, no new modules.
migration_plan: 1. Add _check_email to agent/watchers.py beside _check_keyword (~line 184); ~60 lines: call the existing GmailTools client, gmail_search('is:unread newer_than:1d'), collect message ids.
2. Persist seen ids in the watcher's existing state row (same pattern the url/keyword watchers use for their last-seen state).
3. Route new messages through _analyzed_fire with sender/subject/snippet so the 6/hour analyze cap and Telegram delivery apply unchanged.
4. Register 'email' in the watcher add/list/remove dispatch so 'watcher add email inbox' works.

5. Test: testing/test_watcher_email.py with a fake gmail client - first poll returns 2 unread ids (expect 2 fires), second poll returns the same ids (expect 0 fires). No real Gmail calls: stays in verify's non-costly set.
6. python scripts/verify.py PASS. Manual e2e (with T-258): self-email -> Telegram alert.
risk_notes: Gmail OAuth token refresh is the demo-day risk - exercise the flow early, not the night before. Poll rate is the watcher loop's 60s tick with the 6/hr analyze cap, far under Gmail API quota. Keep the query string a constant, not a config knob, until someone actually needs a different one.
